LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE'S MOTION TO SEAL CERTIFICATES OF MERIT. GRANT. NO OPPOSITION FILED, AND GOOD CAUSE SHOWN PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 340.1. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED IN CAMERA THE SEALED CERTIFICATES OF MERIT, AND PURSUANT TO SECTION 340.1(J) FINDS BASED SOLELY ON THOSE CERTIFICATES OF MERIT THAT THERE IS REASONABLE AND MERITORIOUS CAUSE FOR THE FILING OF THE ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS DOE 1 AND DOE 2. ACCORDINGLY, UPON ENTRY OF THE COURT'S ORDER, PLAINTIFF'S DUTY TO SERVE THE DEFENDANTS WITH PROCESS SHALL ATTACH. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-05-2020 Continued To Jan-27-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Seal Certificates Of Merit
Off Calendar Sep-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE's Motion To Seal Certificates Of Merit.

Grant. No opposition filed, and good cause shown pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 340.1. The Court has reviewed in camera the sealed certificates of merit, and pursuant to section 340.1(j) finds based solely on those certificates of merit that there is reasonable and meritorious cause for the filing of the action against Defendants Doe 1 and Doe 2. Accordingly, upon entry of the Court's order, plaintiff's duty to serve the defendants with process shall attach.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
